statement: Responsive layout across screen sizes (NOT fixed at one width). (a) The Builder must scale fluidly across desktop-class screens from a 14-inch MacBook (~1280–1512px) up to an 85-inch TV (4K, ~3840px): the frozen three-row structure and island roles (core.md §10) are preserved, but dimensions, spacing, and typography scale via responsive units (clamp/min-max/rem/%), honoring the §21 density discipline (≤260px repeating card columns) at the small end. The Builder is NOT required on mobile/tablet (desktop-and-bigger only). (b) The Home and Version pages must additionally be fully responsive on mobile and tablet. The CT-2 structural-dimension tokens (--dim-*) are the substrate to make fluid. This corrects the discovery's fixed-1440 interpretation; the original intent — and REQ-FP-D12's own note 'aligns with D-01 responsive direction' — was responsiveness. Does NOT change layout structure (§10) or the density discipline (§21); it makes sizing fluid within them.
